moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
The portfolio will be less volatile and more diversified.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a ~1.5%/month return, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and exit below-stop positions.
Increase QQQI to 22% to blend broad index and income.
Buy TSLA at 8% to add a high-scoring long setup.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce semiconductor sector concentration.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets a ~2.5%/month return, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is removed, and concentration is reduced. Expect moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.

moves.note: Press what is working, high score

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit below-stop positions and reallocate capital.
Buy TSLA at 15% and NVDU at 10% to concentrate in high-scoring setups.
Add CRWD to 15% to press a working, high-scoring position.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in strong setups for stretch growth.
Maintain strict stop discipline to manage increased risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets a ~4%/month return, aiming for stretch growth. It involves higher concentration and potential for increased leverage, leading to higher upside but also significantly larger drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs can decay daily; strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the elevated risk.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% and sell CRWD to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield with a low drawdown.
A cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets a ~1%/month return, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It aims for low drawdown by cutting leverage and high-beta names. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is cash-like income, not a savings account.
